content: I am a graduate student at NYU Tandon School specialising in Cybersecurity. My research interests are Cryptography, Application Security, Network Security. I have worked in software development and application security for Oracle FSGBU. Currently I work as an intern at Tag Cyber LLC.

content:
An introduction to the field of Computer Forensics.
Tools For this course we will be using tools like:
 Autopsy HexWorkshop FTKImager dcfldd VolatilityFoundation OpenStego  Objectives of a Computer Forensic Investigation There are 4 objectives that defines the course of a computer forensic investigation:
 Prepare for an investigation Acquire the data for investigation. Analyze the data. Idnetify evidence and present it in a report.  Types of Investigations There are 2 types of investigations:
 Public  Contextualized in criminal cases Conducted by law enforcement officers Driven by statutes in criminal law Protected by the Fourth Amendment from unauthorized search and seizure.  Private  Contextualized in civil cases Conducted by corporations or organizations Driven by statutes in civil law & organizational policies. Internal policies dictate the investigation.    An efficient forensic investigator should be able to perform both public and private investigations successfully.
Evidence from Forensics Investigation Every evidence must have the following qualities:
 Reproducible and Verifiable  Evidence must be collected by systematic procedures. Chain of custody and evidence locker to prevent tampering. Use a hash utility and make large copies
title: Computer Forensics
